§6Naphtha§f

§aThe mid-weight fraction — reform it into gasoline, crack it into solvents, or fraction it into diesel and heating oil!§f

Naphtha is the second most common product from the §bOil Refinery§f, yielding §e250mB§f per §e1,000mB§f of hot crude oil. It is a flammable, viscous liquid with medium fuel value (§e110 kTU§f / §e165 kHE§f per bucket).

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Refinery§f (primary): §e250mB§f per §e1,000mB§f hot crude oil.
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: Crack §bIndustrial Oil§f → §e60mB§f Naphtha + §e40mB§f Petroleum Gas.
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: Process §bHeating Oil§f → §e500mB§f Naphtha + §e150mB§f Petroleum Gas.
§b• Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generator§f (via Radiolysis): §e60mB§f Naphtha per §e100mB§f Industrial Oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f Naphtha → §e60mB§f Diesel + §e40mB§f Heating Oil.
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: §e1,000mB§f Naphtha → §e500mB§f Reformate + §e150mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e100mB§f Liquid Hydrogen.
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e1,000mB§f Naphtha → §e800mB§f Gasoline.
§b• Industrial Mixer§f: §e500mB§f Naphtha + §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons → §e1,000mB§f Solvent.
§b• Industrial Solidification Machine§f: §e16,000mB§f Naphtha → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Coker Unit§f: §e4,900mB§f Naphtha → §e1×§f Petroleum Coke + §e490mB§f Coker Naphtha.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e6,500mB§f Naphtha → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.

§eTip§f: Naphtha's fuel value is significantly higher than Heavy Oil. When in doubt, fraction it down to Diesel for the best fuel efficiency, or reform it for more valuable output products.
